title: Entropy
definition: Entropy is a measure of disorder or randomness in a system. In thermodynamics, the change in entropy (ΔS) equals heat transfer (Q) divided by temperature (T) for reversible processes: ΔS = Q/T.

proofs.description: Clausius formulation of second law of thermodynamics
proofs.reference: Clausius, R. (1865). Mechanical Theory of Heat

difficulty_levels.beginner: Entropy measures how spread out or disordered things are
difficulty_levels.intermediate: Entropy change equals heat transfer divided by temperature
difficulty_levels.expert: Entropy quantifies microscopic configurations consistent with macroscopic state
